column27: Comments

Reassign to civilian duty got both sides to savior pretty quickly. 2 retractable swords and helped player 2 a lot, so many tech draw cards. PHD in Oceanography helped player 1. Wolverinepool did escape twice but fortunately he doesn't stay in the escape pile.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Made a tech deck that did good.

Great first game! We quit the second after we had several escapes. Our idea was for us to both be able to play the "hero class matters" kind of deck. Ultimately it was a solid strategy, quite fun. Though the first few master strikes had us nervous.

On the second play through I got lucky with Stingray’s rare in the HQ and a 5 and 1 hand. I got the 3 recruit stingray on turn 1 and an officer on turn 3 while being able to fight a Mutate. Turn 5 I had enough recruit to get his rare with the extra draw, and then it became a rush of how quickly I could KO as much as possible with Stingray and Groot (21 KOs at the end). Built up a very nice Tech ranged side on my left and was working with artifacts on the right. I had the ability to end this a little earlier but would’ve had 1-2 escapes.

This one was hard. Took a lot of turns in the first game but was able to win against the mastermind. The second round I was not lucky enough and pulled all the scheme twists by turn 25. Fun but rough.
Kept the hands on Streets and Bank and prayed I wouldnt need to go to the bridge to avoid an escape. Got size changing rolling keeping the classes separate.

First run was tricky using new cards but second run went better as managed to get Queen Storm and Black Knights rates to deal wounds to the Mastermind allowing a combo of Black Panther, Black Cat and Black Knights empowerment ability to finish off the mastermind and move around the city.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: It went as expected
Rough

Ended up getting unlucky escapes right at the beginning of both games from the scheme twists and Baron macabre. Both games ended up taking longer than before. Really don't like black cat or black knight lol. The empower black knight was good situationally.

One deck was the aggressor, keeping the city nice and clear before moving on one the mastermind. The picked up all the recruit boosting cards and than manipulated the HQ to keep it all one class for empowered based cards. Lot of decisions that needed to be made on every turn, was really complicated but a lot of fun
Brought Queen Storm to make Black Bolt somewhat useful(Controlling the Throne's Favor, and ranged support). Then Princess Shuri came in to bring in more ranged support, as well as HQ manipulation.

Arguing over the Throne’s Favor proved slightly inefficient and I focused primarily on keeping escapes low with the lurking tornado. But finishing off Killmonger was a grind and I think my turn count is gonna do me in.
This scheme is definitely not our fav.
B.F.G. FTW!!! Recruited early, played it twice, each time triggering its special ability AND fighting the mastermind.

I went into this setup not adequately considering Illuminati and its hard nerf on draw. I saw Iceman in the setup and just wanted to ramp up with him. My general idea was to have Bishop and Iceman draw a bunch and provide resources before having Jean Grey's rare give me a bunch of bystanders. This, as I learned in the first game when Jean Grey's rare and most of Iceman's commons were KO'd by the scheme, was a foolish plan. But I stuck with it anyway and luckily on the second go was more-or-less able to get the strategy I wanted. Illuminati was extremely annoying and I had to monitor them closely, Sentinel Squad almost caused a couple escapes. It was a nerve-wracking game, but way more successful than my first.
Hulk is King! and he owns Illiuminati as it should be based on comics :D
Been awhile since I played new mutants

Ranged deck versus Multiple Man, which somehow paired with Outwit, that worked seamlessly somehow. Both sides pulled their weight, and in fact the Outwit side managed to Transfirm 4 of Cho's cats in the same turn, hitting the MM twice!!

column27: With the Illuminati on their transformed side, player 2 generated an infinite loop of drawing and pitching Angel's Diving Catch to clear out the Bystander stack. I'm not sure if I could have repeated the effect with a second playthrough, so although my 1st run wasn't the cleanest I felt like it was good enough to submit immediately. Hopefully the VP I earned will pull me to the top.
This setup seems to boil down to 'can you get Iceman's set to go off' If the answer is yes, congratulations on your win.  If not, you're gonna have a bad time

Gosh what a brutal setup. Bad luck in both attempts was enough to make me lose for sure. Got real close on the second attempt but just ran out of time. Week five is my new record though so I'm happy! GG to everyone!

Fun game. Figured that there'd be wounds galore, so smash effects and/or KO's would come in handy. Got lucky on Lady Sif's rare and that helped carry the day. 2nd setup was a disaster. I couldn't keep the city clear and the twists just wouldn't stop.
Probably better choices, but couldn’t pass up the chance to run the theme.

Pretty fun setup although it seemed to take forever. First attempt was over 40 turns. Using Warpath, Korg for investigate and draw allowed me to recycle Sif's thrown artifacts to great success. MM was meh, i thought he would work better with Moon Knight but it was not as effective as I thought it would be.

Since this is the E Bracket, I just went with what heroes looked fun together. Beta Ray Bill was great with the discard theme from WWH. I didn't get Stormbreaker to show until game 2, but when I did it was great. First game was faster though, so I entered that one.
